records.ety: [L. speciosusgood-looking, beautiful, specious, fr. species look, show, appearance; cf. F. spcoeux. See Species.]

defs: Presenting a pleasing appearance; pleasing in form or look; showy.
quotes: Some [serpents] specious and beautiful to the eye.
The rest, far greater part, Will deem in outward rites and specious forms Religion satisfied.

defs: Apparently right; superficially fair, just, or correct, but not so in reality; appearing well at first view; plausible; as, specious reasoning; a specious argument.
quotes: Misled for a moment by the specious names of religion, liberty, and property.
In consequence of their greater command of specious expression.
